title: Markens grøde, Anden del
summaries: "Markens grøde, Anden del" by Knut Hamsun is the second book of a novel published in 1917 that won the Nobel Prize in Literature. It continues the story of Isak and his family at their Norwegian farm, Sellanraa, as they navigate the tensions between traditional agrarian life and encroaching modernity. The narrative shifts to follow new settler Axel Ström while chronicling unexpected financial reversals, land sales, and the evolving fates of Isak's sons in a changing rural world.
